articles.title: League 42 changes policies after allowing sex offender to coach youth baseball team - KAKE
articles.search_term: ("heat advisory" OR "heat action plan" OR "heat alert" OR "IMD warning" OR "IMD alert" OR "red alert" OR "orange alert" OR "yellow alert" OR "NDMA advisory" OR "cooling shelter" OR "relief measures" OR "heat compensation" OR "severe weather warning") Lakshadweep
articles.full_text:
WICHITA, Kan. (KAKE) -- New policies are in place at League 42 in Wichita after parents learned a sex offender was allowed to coach children with the Little League organization for years and pushed for change.
League 42 says the decision to allow that coach into the program was made years ago, adding he served without any known mistreatment or abuse of players. The organization's board says the coach is no longer on staff, but some parents still feel uneasy.

articles.title: Heatwave in Paris: has the red alert finally been lifted? - Sortir à Paris
articles.search_term: ("heat advisory" OR "heat action plan" OR "heat alert" OR "IMD warning" OR "IMD alert" OR "red alert" OR "orange alert" OR "yellow alert" OR "NDMA advisory" OR "cooling shelter" OR "relief measures" OR "heat compensation" OR "severe weather warning") Lakshadweep
articles.full_text:
The orange heatwave alert replaces the red alert in Paris and across the entire Île-de-France this Sunday, June 28, 2026, and will stay in place this Monday, June 29. Since 6 a.m., Météo-France has nudged the capital, the inner ring (Hauts-de-Seine, Seine-Saint-Denis, Val-de-Marne) and the outer ring (Yvelines, Essonne, Seine-et-Marne, Val-d’Oise) one alert level down, after several days at the highest level. The thunderstorm risk, meanwhile, has dropped to yellow vigilance.
The highest level of the heatwave plan is not based solely on daily temperatures: it also takes into account the duration of the episode, its intensity, and the burden on the health system. In Île-de-France, the heat had been persisting since June 21, hospitals were at full capacity, and the nights stayed suffocating.
This Sunday, the hottest air is shifting to the east of the country, while cooler air advances from the west and northwest. Météo-France has thus moved Paris (75) and all the Île-de-France departments from red to orange, effective from 6 a.m. The orange alert still calls for caution, but it clearly marks the start of a lull in the heat.
It's the other major highlight of the weekend. After the violent thunderstorms on Saturday, which had placed the region under an orange alert and forced the closure of several parks, the risk is subsiding. Île-de-France remains under yellow thunderstorm alert this Sunday, with the core of storm activity now centered from the Pyrenees to the Massif Central.
These showers, the first since the episode began, brought welcome relief. We stay vigilant into the evening, as storms could bring hail, strong gusts and sudden downpours as they move northeast: avoid sheltering under trees and secure anything that could be blown off balconies and terraces.
That's the question everyone is asking. This Sunday, June 28 marks a turning point: highs expected to retreat to around 34 to 36°C in Île-de-France (a regional figure, to be distinguished from the national records of June 24 and 25—the hottest days ever recorded in France). The stormy weather has begun to break the first heatwave of the summer 2026.
Careful, however: homes overheated for more than a week will take time to cool down, and temperatures remain above seasonal norms. To follow the evolution hour by hour, we keep an eye on the carte de vigilance Météo-France, updated twice daily. One question remains for what’s next: la canicule va-t-elle revenir en juillet ?
The heatwave has knocked out several marquee weekend events. Under pressure from the police prefect, who warned organizers to pull the plug or face an official ban, the Paris Pride March (which was planned to run from Place d'Italie to Place de la République), the Solidays festival at Longchamp Racecourse, and the Charléty athletics meeting were canceled this weekend.
The Pride March will nevertheless be postponed to a later date, while Solidays, forgoing its 28th edition, are arranging ticket refunds. The Solidarité Sida association, which the festival funds, has warned about its situation in the wake of this last-minute cancellation.
The heatwave came with an episode of ozone pollution. According to Airparif, the Île-de-France air-quality monitoring group, concentrations exceeded the usual thresholds. This is why the differentiated circulation scheme was put in place by the police prefecture: a measure tied to the pollution peak, not to the heatwave vigilance level. It does not automatically disappear with the orange alert and remains in effect, every day from 5:30 a.m. to 11:59 p.m., until the prefecture decides to lift it.
Until this restriction is lifted, only vehicles displaying a Crit'Air 0, 1 or 2 badge are allowed to circulate within the A86 perimeter, with speeds reduced by 20 km/h. Cooler air and thunderstorms are expected to push ozone levels down, which should lead to an easing of the measure soon. To check whether your vehicle is affected, visit certificat-air.gouv.fr, and monitor announcements from the police prefecture.
On the travel front, the searing heat has again led to train cancellations on several Île-de-France lines: it's wise to plan ahead and check the perturbations dans les transports en Île-de-France.
Even at orange alert, the danger remains real: a heatwave can affect the elderly, children, people who are isolated or ill, as well as athletes. The ARS Île-de-France and the Ville de Paris remind the public of the right precautions:
Paris records nearly 1,400 oases of cool freely accessible: shaded parks, museums, libraries, pools and religious buildings. You can locate the nearest ones with our guide to cool spots in Paris. Note that many museums, monuments and cultural venues are still adjusting their hours—it’s best to check before you go. The Canicule Info Service helpline (0 800 06 66 66) remains reachable from 9 a.m. to 7 p.m., and it’s wise to look in on elderly or isolated neighbors.
To ride out the heat more smoothly, you can take advantage of the air-conditioned spaces during the summer sales or settle into our top air-conditioned restaurants in Paris. To go further, consult all of our information, recommendations and good deals in the face of the heat.
